deferrals.summary: Dedupe-vs-resume race: the auto-resume comment is posted while the original run is still in_progress, so the new run's gate dedupe may classify it as a duplicate and skip — the resume becomes a visible-but-inert audit comment. Needs a live cloud run to confirm timing; a fix likely needs dedupe to exclude a run alive only through its own trailing backstop step. Fold in: consider !cancelled() vs always(), and checking the App token before posting so inert resumes do not consume cap attempts.
deferrals.explanation: Named in issue 268's own body as a boundary to confirm on a live cloud run before designing a dedupe carve-out; the accepted race is documented in the step comment and docs/implement-skill.md. Not the widens-surface pattern: the behavior is new in this PR and explicitly scoped for post-merge live validation by the issue.

deferrals.summary: An unknown/corrupted status glyph classifies as interim rather than unreadable, so a hand-mangled Status line reads as a stall (bounded by the resume cap, fails toward a visible resume/exhaustion).
deferrals.explanation: cmd_status is a #266 primitive shipped in PR #267; this PR's diff does not touch scripts/workpad.py (git diff origin/main...HEAD --name-only confirms).
